_source: Jesse Schell 'The Art of Game Design', GDC talks, Sid Meier, Will Wright
_note: Long-tail design philosophy data. Specific principles with practical application.

flow_state.mihaly_csikszentmihalyi: Optimal experience between anxiety and boredom
flow_state.challenge_skill_balance: Game must continuously calibrate difficulty to player skill growth
flow_state.practical_implementation: Track death count per section; auto-adjust if >5 deaths (offer skip or reduce HP of enemy)

sid_meier_interesting_decisions.rule: A game is a series of interesting decisions
sid_meier_interesting_decisions.bad_decision: Obvious choice (one option is clearly best) or meaningless choice
sid_meier_interesting_decisions.good_decision: Tradeoffs with no clear winner; depends on context, player style, future plans

juiciness.definition: Maximum feedback for minimum input; every action has sound, visual, haptic response
examples: Coin collect: sound + sparkle + +1 float text + counter increment
Enemy death: hitstop + ragdoll + drop items + score popout + rumble
juiciness.overuse_warning: Too much juice = sensory overload; pause after major events

onboarding_design.never_say_never_tutorial: Teach by doing, not by wall of text
onboarding_design.mario_world_1-1_model: Environment teaches mechanics; goomba at safe distance to teach jump
onboarding_design.contextualize_every_mechanic: Introduce in safe environment before combat use

progression_feel.power_fantasy: Player must feel more powerful each session, even if just marginally
progression_feel.visible_progress_bar: Progress bars in UI trigger dopamine; use for XP, quest completion, crafting
progression_feel.unexpected_reward: Slot machine effect — variable reward schedule creates engagement; random rare drops

game_feel_basics.hitstop: Brief (3-6 frame) pause on impact; makes hits feel heavy
game_feel_basics.animation_anticipation: Wind-up before swing; tells player what's coming
game_feel_basics.squash_and_stretch: Deforms objects on impact/jump; adds life and bounce

telegraphing.rule: Player must always be able to react to an attack they haven't seen before
telegraphing.animation_budget: 100-300ms warning before hitbox active; longer for slower player mobility
telegraphing.visual_telegraph: Red glow/color change + warning sound cue
telegraphing.dark_souls_model: Long wind-ups; punish reaction time; learnable with practice

ai_behavior_patterns.finite_state_machines: Idle → Patrol → Alert → Attack → Retreat; most common basic AI
ai_behavior_patterns.behavior_trees: Hierarchical; checks conditions in order; Unity/Unreal standard
ai_behavior_patterns.goal_oriented_action_planning: GOAP: agent has goals, finds action sequences; F.E.A.R. used this, felt emergent
ai_behavior_patterns.flocking: Boids algorithm; 3 rules (separation, alignment, cohesion); natural horde movement

difficulty_archetypes.fodder: Low HP; dies in 1-2 hits; teaches basic combat; cannon fodder for crowds
difficulty_archetypes.bruiser: High HP; slow; telegraphed heavy attacks; damage sponge
difficulty_archetypes.ranged: Forces movement; breaks player comfort zone of standing still
difficulty_archetypes.elite: Mini-boss tier; unique mechanic; requires specific counter-strategy
difficulty_archetypes.boss: Multiple phases; unique arena mechanic; narrative weight

predatory: FOMO-inducing limited-time offers on children's games

gacha_mechanics.pity_system: Guaranteed pull at N attempts; reduces worst-case frustration; industry standard
gacha_mechanics.sparkle_rate: 0.6-1% legendary rate typical; psychological pressure around pity
gacha_mechanics.regulation: Belgium/Netherlands banned loot boxes as gambling; comply with local law
